message:
feat(migrations): implement startup migration system for legacy Pi layouts (bd-35pnc)

Adds src/migrations.rs (622 lines) — a complete startup migration system
that automatically upgrades users' config and session layouts when
switching from pi-mono TypeScript to pi_agent_rust. This is critical for
seamless adoption: without it, users lose API keys, session history, and
custom prompt templates.

Migrations implemented (all idempotent, safe to run repeatedly):

1. AUTH MIGRATION (oauth.json + settings apiKeys → auth.json)
   - Detects legacy oauth.json with per-provider tokens
   - Detects API keys embedded in settings.json under apiKeys
   - Merges both sources into the canonical auth.json format
   - Renames originals to .bak (preserves data, prevents re-migration)

2. SESSION DIRECTORY MIGRATION
   - Moves *.jsonl session files from the agent root directory
     (~/.pi/agent/*.jsonl) to per-project session directories
     (sessions/<encoded-cwd>/) using encode_cwd() for path safety
   - Preserves session metadata and file contents intact

3. PROMPTS DIRECTORY RENAME (commands/ → prompts/)
   - Checks both global (~/.pi/agent/commands/) and per-project dirs
   - Renames to prompts/ to match current naming convention
   - Logs each rename for user visibility

4. MANAGED TOOLS → BIN MIGRATION
   - Moves fd/rg binaries from tools/ to bin/ directory
   - Only moves recognized managed binaries (fd, rg, fd.exe, rg.exe)

5. DEPRECATION WARNINGS
   - Warns about legacy hooks/ directory (suggests docs link)
   - Warns about legacy tools/ directory with non-managed entries
   - All warnings include actionable URLs

The module exports MigrationReport with structured results:
migrated_auth_providers, migrated_session_files, migrated_commands_dirs,
migrated_tool_binaries, deprecation_warnings, and non-fatal warnings.
The messages() method formats human-readable summaries.

Includes unit tests covering each migration path plus idempotency.
Wired into startup via --no-migrations CLI flag for opt-out.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
